steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Left of the blank: “Two of the most celebrated examples... were completed in 1570 and [blank]” — an independent clause.
Right of the blank: “such allegorical artwork was particularly popular from the 15th through the late 18th centuries” — a separate independent clause.
Structure: independent clause + [blank] + independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two independent clauses. Choice A provides “1712, respectively;” — the missing year, the word “respectively,” and the semicolon needed to separate the two independent clauses.
The other choices either use a comma (creating a comma splice in B), place the semicolon incorrectly (D), or omit the year entirely (C). That matches choice A — A is correct.
